qa_pairs.question: What is the initial step for a PI or any sponsored user to access computational resources from the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI and any sponsored user must first request access to the desired system(s) at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems.

qa_pairs.question: What might be required for some systems before access is granted?
qa_pairs.answer: Some systems will require the acceptance of additional agreements, such as Terms of Use or SLAs, before access can be granted.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a user does not request access to the desired system?
qa_pairs.answer: If this step is missed, users will not be able to log in to the desired system.

qa_pairs.question: When are some storage resources made available in the Default RAP for PIs and sponsored users?
qa_pairs.answer: Some storage resources are made available immediately after creating an Alliance account on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: When are Default RAP storage resources ready for utilization?
qa_pairs.answer: They will be ready for utilization as soon as access to the corresponding system is requested at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems.

qa_pairs.question: Can PIs request additional storage resources without submitting a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, PIs can request additional storage resources in any General Purpose cluster without submitting a RAC application if the default resources are not sufficient.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum amount of project storage PIs can request via RAS without a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs can request up to a maximum of 40 TB of project storage without submitting a RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum amount of nearline storage PIs can request via RAS without a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs can request up to a maximum of 100 TB of nearline storage without submitting a RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: Can additional storage requested via RAS be split across multiple clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, these resources can be requested all in one cluster or split across multiple ones, but the total amount across all clusters must not exceed 40 TB of project storage or 100 TB of nearline storage.

qa_pairs.question: In which project will resources allocated via RAS be available?
qa_pairs.answer: Resources allocated via RAS will be available in the Default Resource Allocation Project (RAP).

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters support RAS storage requests?
qa_pairs.answer: Fir, Nibi, Rorqual, and Narval support RAS storage requests.

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters do not offer RAS storage?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium and HPSS do not have RAS storage available.

qa_pairs.question: Who can request storage resources via RAS?
qa_pairs.answer: Only PIs (not sponsored users) can request storage resources via RAS.

qa_pairs.question: How do PIs request storage resources via RAS?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs must send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca with details of the storage resources needed.

qa_pairs.question: Who can use CPU resources for opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: CPU resources are available for opportunistic use to all research groups with an active Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: How do jobs submitted for opportunistic CPU use typically fare?
qa_pairs.answer: The great majority of jobs submitted this way get executed, although sometimes with a lower priority than jobs submitted with a RAC allocation.

qa_pairs.question: On average, how many core years of CPU resources can research groups use with their Default RAP on each cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Research groups may be able to use, on average, up to 200 core years on each cluster with their Default RAP.

qa_pairs.question: Is the 200 core years for CPU usage a fixed reservation or cap?
qa_pairs.answer: No, 200 core years is a variable target; it is not a reservation nor a cap, allowing for more or less utilization depending on job characteristics and cluster usage.

qa_pairs.question: When might applying for a RAC be a better option for CPU resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Applying for RAC may be the best option for jobs with large memory requirements, or if the amount of CPU resources needed exceeds the minimum required to apply for RAC.

qa_pairs.question: Who can use GPU resources for opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: GPU resources are available for opportunistic use to all research groups with an active Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: Why is the demand for GPUs increasing rapidly?
qa_pairs.answer: The demand for GPUs is increasing quickly with the advances in Artificial Intelligence.

qa_pairs.question: How does GPU resource availability fluctuate throughout the year?
qa_pairs.answer: The availability of GPU resources varies greatly over the year, becoming more limited in periods preceding major conferences.

qa_pairs.question: Is the amount of GPU resources guaranteed for opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the Alliance cannot guarantee any amount of resources available for opportunistic use, especially during times of high demand.

qa_pairs.question: Who can consistently use their allocated GPU amount?
qa_pairs.answer: Users with a RAC award should be able to consistently use their allocated amount of GPU resources.

qa_pairs.question: Who can request access to modest amounts of cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs can request access to modest amounts of cloud resources, and other users with an Alliance account may also be granted access.

qa_pairs.question: How do PIs request cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs must complete a Google form at https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeU_BoRk5cEz3AvVLf3e9yZJq-OvcFCQ-mg7p4AWXmUkd5rTw/viewform with details about the cloud resources needed.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted for questions or assistance with RAS cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: For questions about accessing RAS cloud resources or help, contact cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca.
